chapter_title.en: The Reward for the Ziyārah of Ĥusain (a.s.) on Any Day Other than an Eid and the Day of ‘Arafah

text: 1 حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ عُقْبَةَ عَنْ بَشِيرٍ الدَّهَّانِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (ع) أَيُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ زَارَ الْحُسَيْنَ (ع) عَارِفاً بِحَقِّهِ فِي غَيْرِ عِيدٍ وَ لَا عَرَفَةَ كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ عِشْرِينَ حِجَّةً وَ عِشْرِينَ عُمْرَةً مَبْرُورَاتٍ مُتَقَبَّلَاتٍ وَ عِشْرِينَ غَزْوَةً مَعَ نَبِيٍّ مُرْسَلٍ أَوْ إِمَامٍ عَدْلٍ

en.sayyid-mohsen-al-husayni-al-milani: 1. Muḥammad ibn Ja’far narrated to me from Muḥammad ibn Ḥusain ibn Abil Khaťťāb, from Muḥammad ibn Ismā’īl, from Sāliḥ ibn ‘Uqbah, from Bashīr Al-Dahhān, who said: Abū ‘Abdillāh (Imam Sādiq (a.s.)) said: Allāh will register twenty purified and accepted Hajj and ‘Umrah and the reward of (fighting in) twenty battles with one of Allāh’s messengers or with a Just Imam for any believer who goes to the Ziyārah of Ḥusain (a.s.) knowing his rights on any day other than an Eid and the Day of ‘Arafah. The above Ḥadīth has also been narrated to me through the following chain: My father, from Sa’d ibn ‘Abdillāh, from Aḥmad ibn Muḥammad ibn ‘Isā, from Muḥammad ibn Ismā’īl ibn Bazī’, from Sāliḥ ibn ‘Uqbah, from Bashīr Al-Dahhān, from Abī ‘Abdillāh (a.s.).
